note: Defines all weapon materials: their damage multiplier, cost multiplier, allowed weapon categories, racial origins, and special passive effects.

materials.name: Wooden
materials.description: Light, flexible, and easy to work. Lacks power and durability compared to metal weapons.

rare_quality_bonus.description: Rare Wooden weapons carry secondary nature/Organia damage alongside their base Blunt physical damage.

materials.flavor: Sturdy enough to serve but not built to last.

materials.name: Bone
materials.availability: Common in Boglands biome. Rare elsewhere.
materials.description: Sharpened and dried creature bones. Quality on par with Iron but brittle under sustained force. Found primarily in Swampbrood craftsmanship.

rare_quality_bonus.description: Rare Bone weapons sometimes carry a secondary poisonous effect from residual creature toxins in the bone.

materials.flavor: Fashioned from the remains of the bog. Light and fast, but prone to cracking.

materials.name: Stone
materials.availability: Gathered rather than forged. Near-worthless as currency.
materials.description: Crude but effective. Heavier than iron for the same volume. Only practical for simple bludgeoning shapes.

special_passive.description: Stone weapons deal 30% more damage than ordinary Iron clubs of the same type.

materials.flavor: Primitive but heavy. Requires real strength to swing effectively.

materials.name: Iron
materials.description: The baseline metal of Leonoria. Reliable and affordable. No special properties.
materials.flavor: The workhorse of the forge. Nothing special — just dependable.

materials.name: Steel
materials.availability: Common in settled regions and trade cities
materials.description: Refined from iron. Harder, sharper edge retention, and more resilient. The standard for professional fighters.
materials.flavor: A step above iron. Holds its edge longer and strikes harder.

materials.name: Master Blacksmith Steel
materials.availability: Specialist smiths in major cities and guild towns
materials.description: Folded and worked by master craftsmen. Superior edge, balance, and durability. The hallmark of a professional weapon.
materials.flavor: Only a true master of the forge can work this metal. The difference is felt in every strike.

materials.name: Northern Iron
materials.availability: Only made by Northerner blacksmiths. Rare outside the Dark Forest biome.
materials.description: A cold-worked iron alloy developed by Northerner smiths in the harsh conditions of the Dark Forest. Slightly superior to Steel in quality.

special_passive.condition: Any Slashing or Piercing attack with a Northern Iron weapon always applies a Bleed effect.
special_passive.description: Northern Iron always causes a Bleed secondary effect on any successful Slash or Pierce hit.

materials.tooltip_note: Northern Iron — always causes Bleed on slash or pierce
materials.flavor: Forged in the cold of the Northern Kingdoms. The metal carries the bite of the Dark Forest winter.

materials.name: Dark Steel
materials.display_description: Dark olive color with a dull, muted shine
materials.availability: From the Forgotten Kingdom. Rare outside its ruins.
materials.description: A dark olive-colored metal with a dullish shine, mined in the depths of the Forgotten Kingdom. Known for a persistent venomous quality even in unenchanted form.

special_passive.description: All Dark Steel weapons always carry a secondary Venomous damage effect.

materials.tooltip_note: Dark Steel — always causes Venomous secondary damage
materials.flavor: From the depths of the Forgotten Kingdom. Even unenchanted, it carries something foul.

materials.name: Arcanium
materials.display_description: Purple-hued metal with a faint inner glow
materials.availability: Traded in Archon settlements and rare arcane markets
materials.description: A rare metal with a distinctive purple hue. Quality matches Ancient Steel. Resonates with the pain response of living targets.

special_passive.description: 50% chance of causing an Extra Painful Strike on any successful hit.

materials.tooltip_note: Arcanium — 50% chance of Extra Painful Strike
materials.flavor: Humming with a low resonance. Its victims describe the pain as impossible to ignore.

materials.name: Ancient Steel
materials.availability: Only in objects of Archon race origin
materials.description: Silver-toned metal worked only by the Archons. Maximum quality of conventional weapons. Carries a trace of holy light in its forging.

special_passive.description: Always carries a secondary Blessed Materium damage effect.

materials.tooltip_note: Ancient Steel — secondary Blessed Materium damage
materials.flavor: Crafted with techniques lost to all but the Archons. The blade seems to carry light within it.

materials.name: Ilithium
materials.display_description: Bronze-colored dull metal of unusual density
materials.availability: Only in objects of Stone Folk origin
materials.description: A dense bronze-colored metal exclusive to Stone Folk smiths. Maximum quality. Delivers exceptional concussive force, especially in heavy blunt weapons.

special_passive.description_standard: 1H and non-blunt-2H: 50% chance of causing a Concussion effect on hit.
special_passive.description_2h_blunt: 2H Blunt weapons: instead of Concussion, 25% chance to deal secondary Impact damage equal to the raw Blunt physical damage output.

materials.tooltip_note: Ilithium — 50% Concussion chance (2H blunt: 25% Impact secondary instead)
materials.flavor: Worked only by Stone Folk. Strikes with this metal resonate through bone and stone alike.

materials.name: Voidsteel
materials.display_description: Black, obsidian-like metal with a cold shine
materials.availability: Only in objects of Dark One origin
materials.description: Black as void and unnervingly cold. Exclusive to the Dark Ones. Maximum quality. Void damage replaces its primary damage type.

special_passive.description: Voidsteel weapons deal Darkvoid as their primary damage type rather than physical damage.

materials.tooltip_note: Voidsteel — primary damage type is Darkvoid (Shadow Arts)
materials.flavor: Cold to the touch even in summer. The Dark Ones do not forge steel — they summon it from somewhere else.
